title: Lift State Up
description:
`Temperature` computes Fahrenheit from a `celsius` value it holds in state — but the `CelsiusInput` keeps its OWN local state, so the parent never sees what the user types and the result stays at "—".

Lift the state up: make `CelsiusInput` a controlled component that reads `props.value` and calls `props.onChange`, and have the parent pass `value={celsius}` and an `onChange` that updates `celsius`.
objectives: In CelsiusInput, use `props.value` and `props.onChange` instead of local state
In Temperature, render `<CelsiusInput value={celsius} onChange={(e) => setCelsius(e.target.value)} />`
hints: When two components need the same data, move that state to their closest common parent.
The child becomes "controlled": its value comes from props, and changes are reported up via a callback prop.
